postData.contentHtml:
The entertainment world mourns the loss of veteran actor James Handy, who has tragically passed away at the age of 81. Known for his memorable performances in films like 'Top Gun: Maverick' and 'K-9', as well as the acclaimed television series 'NYPD Blue', Handy's death has been officially ruled a homicide by the Los Angeles County Medical Examiner's Office.

According to medical examiner records, Handy died as a result of a stab wound to the torso and neck compression. The incident occurred on June 3 outside a residence in Tarzana, where Handy resided with his girlfriend, Wendy Gledhill, and her son, Michael Gledhill.

Michael Gledhill, 44, has been arrested and charged with murder in connection with Handy's death. Disturbing details surrounding the event include a 911 call where the caller allegedly stated, "I am the son of man, I just killed the man of sin." Upon the arrival of law enforcement, Gledhill reportedly flagged down officers and identified himself as the individual they were seeking. Handy was discovered in the front yard suffering from a stab wound and was later pronounced dead at the hospital.

The District Attorney's office emphasized the senselessness of the act, stating, "This is not how anyone's life should end, stabbed in the chest and left dying in the front yard of a home." The statement highlighted Handy's right to live out his later years in peace.

Social media sentiment regarding James Handy's death and the ongoing homicide investigation is largely sympathetic, with some public debate surrounding the tragic circumstances.
postData.title: Tragedy Strikes: 'Top Gun: Maverick' Actor James Handy's Death Ruled Homicide
postData.summary: Veteran actor James Handy, known for his roles in 'Top Gun: Maverick' and 'NYPD Blue', has tragically died as a result of a homicide. Authorities have charged Michael Gledhill, the son of Handy's girlfriend, in connection with the fatal stabbing.

sources.title: Slain Top Gun: Maverick Actor James Handy's Cause of Death Revealed
